title: Fix child EC2 firmware upload
description: Scope: diagnose and repair only child fleet instance provisioning and firmware/source upload for heterogeneous validation. After .56, use AWS_PROFILE=personal and AWS_REGION=us-west-2 in personal account 210337117346; launch only ARM64 fleet AMI ami-0764d1b512e22671f with Project=LICHEN, unique RunId, LaunchedBy, and Name tags. Never attach approved builder EBS vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb. Because the AMI has tools but no checkout or credentials, package the exact clean source base plus identified binary patch/tool/model inputs, hash locally, transfer with explicit SSH/Instance Connect/SCP timeout and host-key policy, hash remotely, and reject any mismatch before execution. Preserve useful existing SSH key injection, SCP, timeout, and ELF-upload diagnostics.
design: Prove account/region/AMI architecture and ownership tags before launch. Record exact key-generation/injection, reachability polling, upload, remote SHA-256, permission, invocation, and teardown commands with cwd/environment, UTC timing, exit statuses, CLI/OpenSSH/tool versions, instance IDs and RunId, and hashed local/remote logs. Expired key, polling timeout, partial SCP, remote path mismatch, ELF/source/hash mismatch, architecture mismatch, fallback transport, ignored nonzero exit, missing stderr, or retry that erases first failure fails closed. Copy/hash diagnostics first; re-query account and Project/RunId/LaunchedBy ownership, terminate only recorded instances, and wait terminated. Confirm vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb is unattached to fleet; an accidental approved-volume attachment is itself failure and requires sync/unmount, ownership-checked detach, and wait-volume-available before termination.
acceptance_criteria: A newly tagged ami-0764d1b512e22671f child receives the exact recorded source/patch/tool bundle and firmware ELF within explicit bounded time; local and remote SHA-256 values match; remote file type/architecture and executable invocation succeed; every command, timeout, exit, instance ID, RunId and UTC log hash is retained. No fallback, partial transfer, ignored failure, reduced artifact, or unbounded poll passes. Cleanup is account/tag/RunId ownership checked and waits for termination; fleet never uses vol-0a95eee8d1d8461eb, and any accidental attachment is unmounted, detached, and waited available before cleanup can pass.
notes: Preserved original objective: verify SSH key injection, SCP transfer, explicit timeout/failure behavior, and successful ELF upload to temporary Zephyr/Renode fleet instances.
